mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating earnings upgrade cycles and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over 60-day windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production increase exceeding 0.5 percent indicates robust manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which immediately lifts corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns average below zero during triggered months, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently above 2.5 percent reflects underlying economic momentum that supports corporate profit expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a six-month horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant difference between high-GDP and low-GDP regimes (p > 0.05), or if the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.01 over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional correlation between inflation readings and subsequent SPX returns remains above 0.05 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening that accelerates wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to predict returns better than a random walk, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real personal savings exceeding 2.0 percent signals consumer deleveraging and reduced discretionary spending capacity, which contracts corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if forward returns remain positive during triggered periods, or if the predictive power of savings drawdowns vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window.
